examples.title: Calculate Image Histogram
examples.description: Algorithm to compute intensity distribution of grayscale image
examples.code:
ALGORITHM ComputeHistogram(image[M][N])
BEGIN
 // Initialize histogram array for 256 levels
 CREATE array hist[0..255] = {0}
 
 // Count frequency of each intensity level
 FOR i = 0 TO M-1 DO
 FOR j = 0 TO N-1 DO
 intensity = image[i][j]
 hist[intensity] = hist[intensity] + 1
 END FOR
 END FOR
 
 RETURN hist
END
examples.explanation: Iterates through each pixel, reads its intensity value, and increments corresponding histogram bin. Time complexity O(M×N).

examples.title: Pixel Distance Calculations
examples.description: Compute Euclidean, Manhattan, and Chebyshev distances between two pixels
examples.explanation: Three distance metrics used in image processing for connectivity, path finding, and shape analysis.

examples.title: Find 4-Neighbors and 8-Neighbors
examples.description: Algorithm to determine all neighboring pixels for a given pixel position
examples.code:
ALGORITHM FindNeighbors(pixel[x][y], M, N)
BEGIN
 // 4-connected neighbors (N, S, E, W)
 four_neighbors = []
 IF x > 0 THEN four_neighbors.add(pixel[x-1][y]) // North
 IF x < M-1 THEN four_neighbors.add(pixel[x+1][y]) // South
 IF y > 0 THEN four_neighbors.add(pixel[x][y-1]) // West
 IF y < N-1 THEN four_neighbors.add(pixel[x][y+1]) // East
 
 // 8-connected neighbors (includes diagonals)
 eight_neighbors = four_neighbors
 IF x > 0 AND y > 0 THEN 
 eight_neighbors.add(pixel[x-1][y-1]) // NW
 IF x > 0 AND y < N-1 THEN 
 eight_neighbors.add(pixel[x-1][y+1]) // NE
 IF x < M-1 AND y > 0 THEN 
 eight_neighbors.add(pixel[x+1][y-1]) // SW
 IF x < M-1 AND y < N-1 THEN 
 eight_neighbors.add(pixel[x+1][y+1]) // SE
 
 RETURN four_neighbors, eight_neighbors
END
examples.explanation: Boundary checks ensure we don't access pixels outside image. Used in region growing, blob detection.

examples.explanation: Important for understanding storage requirements and planning memory allocation for image processing applications.
